avoid_when: the user is still deciding what product to build
the task is only visual direction without implementation
the task is legal, accounting, or domain research without code
use_when: the user asks to write, debug, test, refactor, deploy, or review code
the workflow has already clarified product/design intent and now needs implementation
technical verification or regression coverage is required

avoid_when: the task is only code implementation
the task is only visual styling after product scope is fixed
the task only needs raw factual research
use_when: the user asks for product judgment, PRD, roadmap, MVP, user stories, positioning, or prioritization
research evidence must become product scope or product decisions
a workflow needs a product owner before design or implementation

avoid_when: the user already has enough evidence and needs a decision
the task is pure implementation
the task is final copywriting without research uncertainty
use_when: the user asks for investigation, market research, competitor analysis, evidence, or comparison
another Agent needs a fact base before planning, product decision, or writing
the task depends on uncertain assumptions that should be verified first

avoid_when: a single specialist can safely complete the task without clarification
the user explicitly requests an existing specialist Agent
use_when: the task is unclear, ambiguous, or needs intent clarification
the user asks to hire, request, deploy, or improve an Agent
the task crosses approval, knowledge, memory, routing, or workflow boundaries
the router has low confidence or multiple plausible owners

avoid_when: the user only needs product prioritization
the user only needs code implementation after a finished design
the task is writing or market research
use_when: the user asks for UI, UX, visual design, prototype, design review, or skeuomorphic interface work
PM output needs to become interaction flows or visual direction
the task concerns GUI consistency, usability, accessibility, or visual quality

avoid_when: the user needs product strategy before writing
the user needs fresh evidence gathering before drafting
the task is UI design or code implementation
use_when: the user asks for articles, posts, copywriting, editing, or voice refinement
research or product notes need to become publishable text
the task is about tone, structure, readability, or audience fit

avoid_when: the user only needs factual research
the main task is writing final copy
the main task is coding without planning ambiguity
use_when: the user needs a plan, architecture, roadmap, feasibility path, or implementation sequence
research output must be turned into an actionable plan
the work needs scope, milestones, dependencies, or risk ordering

avoid_when: the user asks for litigation representation, court filing, or final legal opinion without professional human review
the request can be handled directly by a non-legal specialist without legal risk
the task requires jurisdiction-specific legal advice but no source documents or jurisdiction are provided
use_when: the user asks the enterprise legal department to review, triage, or coordinate a legal or compliance matter
the task needs legal-risk framing before another specialist proceeds
multiple legal capability lanes must be synthesized into one business-facing recommendation draft

avoid_when: the request is primarily privacy, data, employment, IP, or regulatory without a contract artifact
the user requests final legal conclusions instead of an internal review draft
no contract, clause, negotiation history, or review objective is available
primary_outputs: escalation items for human legal review
use_when: the task concerns supplier, customer, SaaS, NDA, procurement, service, or partnership contracts
the user needs contract risk triage, clause review, negotiation positions, or signed-contract playbook deviations
corporate transaction materials need structured diligence or disclosure support

avoid_when: the task is only ordinary product planning without legal or compliance risk
the task is purely contract clause negotiation better handled by legal-contracts
the user asks for external legal opinion without professional human review
primary_outputs: required evidence and human approval list
use_when: the task involves privacy, data protection, AI governance, product launch, marketing claims, employment, IP, or regulatory change
the user needs an internal compliance review draft before business action
the task needs legal-risk classification and source-backed review questions

legal-compliance.display_name: Digital Office Compliance and Product Legal Specialist

description: Canonical registry for the experimental digital-office agent team. Keep domain roles, routing rules, workflows, skills, models, and migration metadata here instead of hard-coding them in the router.

design.description: Owns GUI, UX, visual direction, prototype, accessibility, and design review.

evidence.description: Gathers facts, source material, market/domain evidence, comparisons, and assumptions.

implementation.description: Owns code, debugging, tests, refactoring, deployment, and technical verification.

intake.description: Clarifies ambiguous work, handles approvals, staffing, memory/knowledge boundaries, and workflow dispatch.

planning.description: Turns intent and evidence into plans, architecture, milestones, dependencies, and feasible execution paths.

product.description: Owns product judgment, requirements, prioritization, roadmap, acceptance criteria, and build/no-build decisions.

writing.description: Owns articles, posts, copywriting, editing, and publishable narrative.

delivery.description: Final delivery validation, artifact packaging, user-facing summary, and unresolved assumption reporting.

legal_intake.description: Enterprise legal department intake, jurisdiction/source scoping, matter classification, and approval-boundary setup.

legal_contracts.description: Supplier, customer, SaaS, NDA, procurement, services, corporate contract, and diligence review drafts.

legal_compliance.description: Privacy, data, product launch, marketing claims, AI governance, employment, IP, and regulatory compliance triage.

legal_synthesis.description: Legal department synthesis, business-facing risk framing, unresolved assumptions, and human review gates.

portable.rule: A migration may change paths, credentials, profiles, skills, models, and domain vocabulary, but must not require rewriting the routing framework.

route_tests.prompt: 规划一下产品方案
帮我写代码修 bug
写一篇公众号文章去AI味
调研一下AI办公竞品
做一个拟物化数字办公室 UI 原型
这个数字办公室产品该不该做
先调研竞品，再规划数字办公室的产品方案
先做市场调研，再判断这个产品该不该做和路线图
先明确产品需求，再做拟态 GUI 设计
先梳理产品需求，再设计界面，最后实现前端原型代码
help me write a PRD
frontend design review
我想招聘一个新的 Agent 员工
这个任务我没说清楚，你先帮我澄清需求并安排工作流
新 Agent 已下载部署，帮我生成加入工作流的报告
通过对话改进已有 Agent 的灵魂文档
帮我做一份PPT
做一份6页的产品汇报
I need a presentation deck for the board meeting
Make a slide deck for tomorrow's product review
请法务部审查这份供应商合同，重点看责任限制、解除和赔偿条款
请法务评估这个新功能的个人信息和数据合规风险
产品上线前请法务做营销宣传和AI治理合规审查
请企业法务评估劳动合同解除和开源合规风险
收到律师函了，请法务部做争议分流和应对清单
Please ask the legal department to review this supplier contract, especially limitation of liability, termination, and indemnity.
Ask legal to assess personal information and data compliance risk for this new feature.
Before product launch, ask legal to review marketing claims and AI governance compliance.
Ask enterprise legal to evaluate employment termination and open source compliance risk.
We received a demand letter; ask legal to triage the dispute and prepare response options.

routing_policy.secretary_fallback_rule: Route to secretary when the prompt is low-confidence, ambiguous, asks for staffing/new Agent work, requires user clarification, or crosses policy/approval boundaries.

workflow_routes.reason: The task asks for evidence gathering before a plan, architecture, or implementation path.
The task asks for evidence gathering before product judgment, priority, or roadmap decisions.
The task needs product intent clarified before UI/UX design.
The task asks for end-to-end product clarification, design, and implementation.
Presentation requests need secretary intake, narrative writing, deck design, and secretary delivery validation.
Contract matters should enter the enterprise legal department workflow, with contract specialist review and legal lead synthesis.
Privacy and data matters need compliance specialist review and legal department synthesis before business reliance.
Product, marketing, AI, and regulatory requests need compliance review plus legal lead synthesis.
Employment, IP, and open-source matters enter the compliance lane with legal lead synthesis.
Dispute matters should be triaged internally and prepared for human/external counsel review when needed.
Generic legal department requests start with legal intake before any specialist lane is selected.

pm_to_design.handoff_contract: PM clarifies product intent and user value. Designer turns it into interaction, visual direction, and prototype guidance.
pm_to_design.label: Product to design

pm_to_design_to_code.handoff_contract: PM defines product shape, Designer creates UI direction, Coder implements or reviews the build.
pm_to_design_to_code.label: Product to design to implementation

ppt_production.handoff_contract: Secretary clarifies the presentation intent, audience, format, and acceptance criteria. Writer produces storyline, slide copy, message hierarchy, and speaker notes. Designer produces visual direction, page composition, media decisions, and the renderable deck artifact. Secretary runs final gates and delivers artifact path, URL, or open instructions with unresolved assumptions.
ppt_production.label: PPT production from intent to validated deck

research_then_plan.handoff_contract: Researcher produces key assumptions, first-principles breakdown, and fact chain. Planner must base the plan on that report.
research_then_plan.label: Research before planning

research_then_pm.handoff_contract: Researcher produces the evidence base. PM turns it into product judgment, scope, risks, and decision options.
research_then_pm.label: Research before product decision

single.handoff_contract: Handle the user task inside the selected specialist boundary. Include assumptions, evidence, and next actions when relevant.
single.label: Single specialist

legal_triage.handoff_contract: Legal department lead clarifies jurisdiction, business objective, source materials, risk tolerance, and whether specialist review or human professional review is required. Output is an internal triage draft, not legal advice.
legal_triage.label: Enterprise legal intake and triage

legal_contract_review.handoff_contract: Legal lead scopes the contract matter and review objectives. Contract specialist reviews clauses, source materials, risk positions, and proposed fallbacks. Legal lead synthesizes a business-facing review draft, unresolved assumptions, and human review gates.
legal_contract_review.label: Enterprise contract review

legal_product_compliance_review.handoff_contract: Legal lead scopes product, marketing, AI, or regulatory compliance questions. Compliance specialist runs source-backed issue triage and control checks. Legal lead synthesizes launch/blocker recommendations for human approval.
legal_product_compliance_review.label: Product and compliance legal review

legal_privacy_data_review.handoff_contract: Legal lead scopes data flows, roles, jurisdiction, and artifacts. Compliance specialist reviews privacy, data processing, PIA/DPA, and data-rights risks. Legal lead delivers an internal review draft and required human/legal approval points.
legal_privacy_data_review.label: Privacy and data legal review

legal_employment_ip_review.handoff_contract: Legal lead scopes employment, IP, or open-source issues and required evidence. Compliance specialist builds the issue matrix and source-backed risk flags. Legal lead synthesizes next actions, approvals, and external counsel triggers.
legal_employment_ip_review.label: Employment, IP, and open-source legal review

legal_dispute_triage.handoff_contract: Legal lead scopes the demand, dispute, litigation, or arbitration posture. Contract specialist maps obligations, facts, deadlines, and evidence gaps. Legal lead prepares an internal dispute triage memo and human/external counsel escalation gates.
legal_dispute_triage.label: Dispute and demand-letter triage
